original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: AUDIT: The Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test — Guidelines for Use in Primary Care, Second Edition
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: World Health Organization international guidance requiring UAE service adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults in primary and general healthcare settings where alcohol-use screening or brief assessment is clinically appropriate.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary healthcare and general medical care.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Supports clinician-entered alcohol frequency, quantity, heavy-use episodes, dependence-related symptoms, alcohol-related harm, and screening context without automatically scoring, diagnosing, or generating intervention advice.

exact_sections.heading: Purpose of the manual and context of alcohol screening
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting why alcohol use was reviewed and the primary-care screening context without asserting hazardous use.

exact_sections.heading: AUDIT questions and domains
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording alcohol frequency, typical quantity, heavy-use episodes, dependence-related symptoms, harmful consequences, concern from others, and prior injury without automatically scoring or diagnosing.

title: AUDIT: The Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test — Guidelines for Use in Primary Care, Second Edition
jurisdiction: World Health Organization international guidance requiring UAE service adaptation
population: Adults in primary and general healthcare settings where alcohol-use screening or brief assessment is clinically appropriate.
intended_setting: Primary healthcare and general medical care.
access_limitations: Official host returned a challenge or shell page rather than guideline content.
